exemplar.title: To what extent has a lack of intersectionality in Western feminism influenced the differentiation in literary portrayals of the âmadwomanâ between Black and white female characters?

detailedExemplarData.title: To what extent has a lack of intersectionality in Western feminism influenced the differentiation in literary portrayals of the âmadwomanâ between Black and white female characters?

subject.title: English Lang & Lit (Old)
subject.description: IB English Language & Literature combines the study of language and literature, analyzing both literary and non-literary texts across various media and contexts. Students explore how language shapes meaning, identity, and culture through critical examination of diverse text types including advertisements, speeches, novels, and digital media. The course develops analytical skills, cultural understanding, and critical thinking about communication in different contexts and purposes.

strengths: Topic and research question are articulated clearly and maintained throughout the essay.
Consistent focus on the âmadwomanâ trope across three primary texts.
Conclusion effectively synthesizes findings in direct relation to the research question.
weaknesses: Methodology lacks explicit explanation of selection criteria and uneven balance of textual analysis.
Occasional background digressions dilute concision and direct alignment with the research question.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Two strands sit firmly in the 5â6 band and one in the 3â4 band. The topic is communicated accurately and effectively, and the research question remains central, though methodological exposition and occasional drift prevent a perfect band-6 mark. A band-5 score (5/6) best reflects this high level of focus with minor shortcomings.

strengths: Demonstrates good, coherent knowledge of feminist theory and intersectionality.
Uses subject-specific terminology consistently and mostly accurately.
Varied and extensive bibliography shows thorough research.
weaknesses: Some claims rely on broad generalisations or lack empirical backing.
Occasional slips in citation details and minor terminology errors prevent full precision.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Both strands are assessed at the 3â4 band. The work shows clear and effective understanding of concepts and uses terminology appropriately, but a few unsupported inferences and citation inconsistencies preclude elevating to the 5â6 band. A score of 4/6 is therefore warranted.

strengths: Research draws on an extensive, relevant range of primary and secondary sources.
Insightful comparisons (e.g., coping mechanisms of white vs. Black women) show thoughtful analysis.
Argument about biased representation demonstrates evaluative thinking aligned to the research question.
weaknesses: Analysis often remains at a descriptive or summary level rather than fully close-reading the texts.
Counter-arguments and synthesis across texts are sometimes asserted rather than systematically demonstrated.
A few conclusions lack direct textual support or rely on tangential sources.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Strand scoresâ5 for research relevance, 4 for depth of analysis, and 4 for discussionâaverage to just over the 4â6 bandâs lower boundary. While there is clear strength in source relevance and evaluative intent, limitations in close-text analysis and critical integration keep the criterion at 4/12.

strengths: Basic inclusion of major sections (title page, contents, works cited).
weaknesses: Absence of key EE elements (abstract, explicit methodology section).
Missing page numbers and inconsistent heading/sub-heading use.
Formatting errors such as broken URLs and uneven paragraph layout.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Both structure and layout strands fall within the 1â2 band, but the severity of missing essential components (no abstract, no consistent navigation aids) places the work at the low end of that band. A mark of 1/4 reflects these significant presentation shortcomings.

strandSelections.reasoning: The student outlines a comparative literary methodologyâclose reading of three primary texts supported by scholarly secondary materialâand supplies an extensive works-cited list.  However, the essay gives no explicit explanation of selection criteria, balance across texts is uneven (Jane Eyre receives far more attention than Luster), and citation execution is inconsistent.  These shortcomings show that the approach is âmostly completeâ rather than fully âeffective,â aligning with the 3-4 band.

Formatting shows some strengths: a word count and title appear, in-text citations are given, and the works-cited list follows a broadly MLA-style layout.  However, page numbers are absent in the supplied text, several long URLs break formatting conventions, and citation punctuation is inconsistent.  These partial lapses match the 1-2 band for layout and formatting.

The bibliography is extensive and varied (academic articles, philosophical texts, medical reports, literary criticism).  Most sources directly inform the argument about feminism, race, and mental illness, showing clear relevance.  A few citations appear tangential or are not integrated into analysis, so the research is âmostlyâ rather than âconsistentlyâ pertinent, placing it in the middle of the 4-6 band.

The studentâs topicâhow the absence of intersectionality in Western feminism shapes racialized portrayals of âmadwomenâ in literatureâis stated on the title page, reiterated in the introduction, and remains visible throughout each section.  The exploration of three named primary works and the recurring reference to the âmadwomanâ trope establish a consistent, purposeful focus.  Occasional digressions (e.g., lengthy historical summaries) reduce concision, yet overall clarity and direction meet the descriptor of an âaccurately and effectivelyâ communicated topic.

An overarching argumentâthat lack of intersectionality skews literary depictions of female madnessâis articulated, and the essay attempts evaluation of each textâs cultural impact.  However, critical evaluation is intermittent; counter-arguments are lightly addressed and synthesis across texts is sometimes asserted rather than demonstrated.  The work thus meets the descriptor for âgood argument development with partially critical evaluation.â

The student offers comparative readingsâcontrasting Jane vs. Bertha, Fleabag vs. Edieâand links them to feminist theory.  Yet passages often summarise content or assert social commentary without sustained textual close-reading, and some conclusions (e.g., Fleabagâs influence on activism) are insufficiently evidenced.  Thus analysis is âadequateâ to âgood,â with conclusions mostly but not fully supported.

The essay contains the required major sections (title page, contents, main body, conclusion, works cited) and uses headings, so the basic structure is recognizable.  Nevertheless, elements expected in an EEâabstract, explicit methodology section, consistent sub-headingsâare missing or under-developed, and paragraphing is occasionally uneven.  This aligns with the âgenerally appropriateâ structure of the 1-2 band.

The student demonstrates solid knowledge of feminist theory, intersectionality, and the chosen literary works, drawing on a wide bibliography.  Key concepts (e.g., first-wave feminism, rest cure) are summarised accurately and linked to textual examples.  Yet several claims rely on broad generalisations or speculative racial inferences (e.g., Bertha Masonâs ethnicity), and some secondary sources are invoked only superficially.  Hence knowledge is âgoodâ but not âexcellent.â

Subject-specific termsââintersectionality,â âfirst-wave/second-wave feminism,â âdissociative feminism,â âmadwoman tropeââare employed frequently and mostly correctly.  Occasional slips (e.g., misspelling of âportrayals,â casual phrasing like âje ne sais quoiâ) prevent complete consistency, but the overall use is âmostly accurate,â meriting the 3-4 band.

The research question is explicitly worded on the title page: âTo what extent has a lack of intersectionality in Western feminism influenced the differentiation in literary portrayals of the âmadwomanâ between Black and white female characters?â  It is specific (race-based contrast, feminism, three texts) and remains a clear thread in body paragraphs and the conclusion.  While broad phrases such as âto what extentâ leave scope slightly wide, the connection between the question and the ensuing discussion is consistently maintained, satisfying the âclearly stated, focused and effectively connectedâ descriptor.

annotations.comment: The table of contents accurately lists major sections, reflecting basic structural awareness.
Page numbering is absent; add consistent headers or footers to aid navigation.
The research question is prominently stated on the title page, providing clear focus from the outset.
Including both the title and word count meets formatting expectations and helps situate the reader.
Misspelling âportravalsâ should be corrected to âportrayalsâ to maintain professionalism.
No explicit methodology section is provided; include a concise description of text selection criteria and analytical approach.
Sections in the Table of Contents lack page numbers; include numeric references for clarity.
The generic heading âBackground Informationâ could be more specific to maintain direct alignment with the research question.
The opening background paragraph is engaging but drifts from the central research focus; tighten to underscore the main question.
The informal opener (âIf music has its fingerâ¦â) is vivid but risks an overly casual tone; consider a more academic phrasing.
Demonstrates strong knowledge of historical constructs (Foucault, rest cure) and connects them to feminist literary themes.
This paragraph summarises waves of feminism well but offers limited critique; deepen engagement by evaluating the sourcesâ positions.
Define âfirst-wave/second-wave feminismâ with an authoritative citation (e.g., hooks, 1984) when first introduced.
Clearly states the three primary texts to be analysed, establishing a coherent methodological scope.
Uses a compelling quotation from Jane Eyre (Ch.2, p.14) to illustrate Janeâs emotional state, showing effective close reading.
Ensure parenthetical citations (e.g., Hughes) follow MLA conventions consistently (author page-number).
The âFleabag eraâ connection to TikTok demonstrates originality and awareness of cultural impact.
Paragraphs are lengthy and cover multiple ideas; consider breaking into sub-sections for enhanced readability.
Insightful comparison of coping mechanisms between white and Black women reveals nuanced critical analysis.
Occasional passive constructions weaken impact; revise to active voice to strengthen assertions.
The research question is reiterated with rationale, reinforcing the essayâs clear focus and purpose.
Inferring Bertha Masonâs race from physical description is valid but requires scholarly support; cite a BrontÃ« critic for substantiation.
The claim about TikTok usage lacks empirical support; include a citation to social media research or usage data.
Balance the analysis across all three works by integrating examples from Luster earlier in the section.
The critique of biased representation shows strong evaluative thinking and relevance to the research question.
The discussion of Erica Childsâ study is promising but lacks direct linkage to your argument; explicitly connect findings to your analysis.
Introduce and define âunconscious biasâ with a precise academic citation when first used.
The metaphor comparing oppression to a domino effect is vivid but ambiguous; clarify the causal relationships more precisely.
Include a transition sentence at the end of this paragraph to signal movement toward broader conclusions.
Effectively applies Crenshawâs intersectionality to underscore the necessity of inclusive activism.
The âoppression olympicsâ dialogue is insightful but could be more concise; trim tangential detail to retain focus.
Link this discussion of literatureâs societal impact directly back to the research question before moving to the conclusion.
The conclusion succinctly synthesizes key findings on racialized portrayals, reinforcing the essayâs central argument.
Limitations discussion omits methodological constraints; consider reflecting on text selection or analytical scope.
Future research section could be expanded with concrete proposalsâe.g., examining additional texts or genres.
The analysis of Edieâs narrative in Luster is well synthesized with intersectionality theory, strengthening your argument.
Works Cited entries show inconsistent indentation and line breaks; apply a hanging-indent style and avoid mid-url breaks.
The extensive and varied bibliography reflects thorough research and a strong understanding of sources.
Several citations omit DOIs or publication years; verify each entry for completeness and accuracy.
